benchmark_details.name: THRONE (Text-from-image Hallucination Recognition with Object-probes for open-ended Evaluation)
benchmark_details.overview: THRONE establishes an accurate and accessible benchmark to quantitatively evaluate object hallucinations in free-form responses, leveraging language models to judge the existence of Type I hallucinations.

purpose_and_intended_users.goal: To provide a framework for evaluating Type I hallucinations in large vision-language models by employing multiple language models and a voting mechanism.

data.annotation: Annotations are derived from COCO with object categories established for evaluation.

methodology.calculation: Metrics are calculated based on the performance of large vision-language models in detecting object hallucinations. F0.5 Score favors precision over recall.
methodology.interpretation: Higher F0.5 scores indicate better performance in minimizing hallucinations in model responses.
methodology.validation: THRONE uses a unanimous voting mechanism across multiple language models to ensure robust evaluation.

harm: Potential for misinterpretation of generated responses leading to misinformation.
